---
title: "Rust Quest - Coding Adventures"
subtitle: "Friendly Rust Mini-Lessons for Complete Beginners"
format:
  live-html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# 🦀 Welcome to Rust Quest!

Embark on a beginner-friendly journey into Rust. Each quest is a short adventure with examples, explanations, and gentle challenges.
:::

## 🎯 What is Rust Quest?

Rust Quest is a collection of mini-lessons designed to make Rust feel approachable, practical, and fun. Each quest includes:

- 📖 A friendly introduction and context
- 💡 Clear Rust examples with step-by-step ideas
- 🎯 Gentle challenges for first-time Rust learners
- 👨‍💻 Code snippets you can try in Rust Playground or locally
- ✅ Solutions to check your work

These lessons are built for students who have **never used Rust before**. Questions and feedback are always welcome!

* Contact: Oliver Bonham-Carter
* Email: obonhamcarter at allegheny.edu
  * website: [https://www.oliverbonhamcarter.com](https://www.oliverbonhamcarter.com)
* Allegheny College: Department of Computer and Information Science
  * website: [https://cs.allegheny.edu](https://cs.allegheny.edu)

## 🧪 Ways to Practice Rust

::: {.highlight-box .highlight-info}
**Choose your preferred Rust coding environment:**

1. **🌐 Rust Playground** - Run Rust in your browser ([Open Playground →](https://play.rust-lang.org/))
2. **💻 Local Rust** - Install `rustup` and use `cargo run`
3. **📓 Lesson Notebooks** - Read lesson notebooks with Rust examples and prompts

[Learn more about interactive options →](interactive.qmd)
:::

## 🗺️ Your Rust Quest Map

<div class="quest-grid">

::: {.quest-card}
### 🎒 Quest 1: Variables
**Your Starter Toolkit**

Learn how Rust stores values, and when to use immutable vs mutable variables.

[Start Quest →](lessons/01-variables.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🔷 Quest 2: Data Types
**The Shape of Data**

Discover integers, floats, booleans, chars, and strings in Rust.

[Start Quest →](lessons/02-data-types.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🛤️ Quest 3: Conditionals
**Choose Your Path**

Make decisions with `if`, `else`, and boolean expressions.

[Start Quest →](lessons/03-conditionals.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🔄 Quest 4: Loops
**Repeat with Confidence**

Use `loop`, `while`, and `for` to repeat work safely.

[Start Quest →](lessons/04-loops.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 📦 Quest 5: Vectors
**Collecting Data**

Store and update many values with `Vec<T>`.

[Start Quest →](lessons/05-lists.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### ✨ Quest 6: Functions
**Reusable Steps**

Break code into reusable functions with parameters and return values.

[Start Quest →](lessons/06-functions.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🗝️ Quest 7: HashMaps
**Keys and Values**

Store key-value data using `HashMap`.

[Start Quest →](lessons/07-dictionaries.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🌟 Quest 8: Fibonacci
**Pattern Practice**

Practice loops and functions by generating Fibonacci numbers.

[Start Quest →](lessons/08-fibonacci.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 📊 Quest 9: Sorting
**Organizing Data**

Sort vectors and reason about ordering steps.

[Start Quest →](lessons/09-sorting.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🎲 Quest 10: Number Game
**Guess and Check**

Build a tiny game using loops and decisions.

[Start Quest →](lessons/10-number-game.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🔁 Quest 11: Recursion
**Functions Calling Themselves**

Use base cases and recursive steps safely.

[Start Quest →](lessons/11-recursion.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### ⚡ Quest 12: Complexity
**Why Speed Matters**

Compare simple growth patterns like $O(n)$.

[Start Quest →](lessons/12-complexity.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🔄 Quest 13: Iterative Loops
**Pick the Right Loop**

Choose `for`, `while`, or `loop` with confidence.

[Start Quest →](lessons/13-iterative-loops.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🧩 Quest 14: Functions Part 2
**Non-Returning**

Write clean action functions for output and workflow.

[Start Quest →](lessons/14-functions-non-returning.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🎁 Quest 15: Functions Part 3
**Returning Values**

Build reusable functions that compute and return results.

[Start Quest →](lessons/15-functions-returning.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### ⚡ Quest 16: Closures
**Quick Function Tools**

Use inline closures with iterator pipelines.

[Start Quest →](lessons/16-lambda-functions.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🧠 Quest 17: Higher-Order Functions
**Behavior as Input**

Pass closures into functions for flexible design.

[Start Quest →](lessons/17-higher-order-functions.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🎁 Quest 18: Wrappers
**Add Behavior Around Functions**

Apply decorator-like wrapper patterns in Rust.

[Start Quest →](lessons/18-decorators.qmd)
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🏗️ Quest 19: Structs and Impl
**Rust Blueprints**

Model data and methods with Rust structs.

[Start Quest →](lessons/19-classes.qmd)
:::

</div>

## 📱 Access on Your Phone

Each quest can still be shared by URL or QR code for quick mobile access.

## 🎓 Ready to Start?

Start with Quest 1 and move forward step by step. Rust rewards steady practice.

::: {.tip-box}
**💡 Pro Tip**: Each quest is independent, so you can jump around based on what interests you most. But if you're brand new to coding, starting from Quest 1 is a great idea!
:::

---

<div style="text-align: center; padding: 2rem;">
**Happy Rusting! May your compiler errors teach you something useful.** 🦀✨
</div>
